title: User profiles: open-ended self-description via encrypted broadcast
description: Users want to share profile fields (handle, status, location, gender, etc.) with room members. These fields are sensitive — the relay must never see them. The right design is a ClearMessage::Profile variant that travels as an opaque encrypted payload, identical to chat messages from the relay's perspective. Clients store received profiles in-memory keyed by pub_id and republish their own profile on connect so new joiners see it. Pre-MLS the payload is plaintext-in-envelope (same as chat today); post-MLS it is automatically encrypted with no relay changes required.
